task.title: Migrate source to domain-led CodeWiki layers
task.summary: Restructure CodeWiki source so domain concepts power DDD and application code contains compilers, gateways, state engine, and agent-facing tools over those domain concepts.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki source follows the documented dependency direction: adapters/UI/skills call application tools, application compilers/gateways/state-engine use domain concepts, and domain owns task/roadmap/session/build/validation/state semantics.
acceptance: Domain modules exist for task, roadmap, session queue, build, validation, and state concepts, with product semantics moved out of broad application modules where practical.
Application exposes agent-callable tool APIs that Pi tools, future adapters, skill helpers, and optional CLIs can call without owning semantics.
Application compiler modules exist for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation flows, even if migration starts with wrappers around existing implementations.
Application gateway modules are separated by validation profile and do not collapse into compiler build production.
State-engine naming owns generated state/graph rebuild/query behavior while graph remains the generated representation.
Dependency checks or tests prevent domain importing application/adapters/UI/skills/scripts and prevent application importing adapters/UI/skills/scripts.
non_goals: Do not change user-facing behavior except import/path compatibility required by the refactor.
Do not remove legacy compatibility exports until downstream adapters/tests are migrated.
verification: source-owned architecture/audit check covers dependency direction

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation must not reuse builder thought context and must cite checked content proof.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

summary: Completed TASK-087 domain-led layering migration through domain modules, compiler/gateway wrappers, dependency guards, and compatibility exports.

evidence: audit-drift fixture covers dependency direction guards.
roadmap-task-boundary smoke covers extracted domain task-boundary semantics.
loop-isolation-policy and alignment-graph smoke cover traceability/build lifecycle behavior.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Domain modules now own extracted semantics and constants.
Application compilers/gateway profile modules provide named wrapper seams.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: audit-drift fixture covers dependency direction guards.
roadmap-task-boundary smoke covers extracted domain task-boundary semantics.
loop-isolation-policy and alignment-graph smoke cover traceability/build lifecycle behavior.
code_change_evidence: Domain modules now own extracted semantics and constants.
Application compilers/gateway profile modules provide named wrapper seams.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Domain modules exist for task, roadmap, session queue, build, validation, and state concepts, with product semantics moved out of broad application modules where practical.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Added domain modules for roadmap task boundary/status, change traceability, build lifecycle, session artifact status, validation profiles, and state sections; reused roadmap/change/build domain helpers from application code.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Application exposes agent-callable tool APIs that Pi tools, future adapters, skill helpers, and optional CLIs can call without owning semantics.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Existing application tool APIs remain stable; compatibility re-export preserves src/application/task-boundary.ts and application build/write APIs.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Application compiler modules exist for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation flows, even if migration starts with wrappers around existing implementations.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Added src/application/compilers/{feedback,documentation,planning,implementation}.ts wrappers around existing build writers.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Application gateway modules are separated by validation profile and do not collapse into compiler build production.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Added src/application/gateway/profiles/{implementation,task-close}.ts profile wrapper modules around validation report writer, separate from compiler wrapper modules.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: State-engine naming owns generated state/graph rebuild/query behavior while graph remains the generated representation.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Existing state modules remain as application state-engine surface; added src/domain/state/sections.ts for domain state section taxonomy.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Dependency checks or tests prevent domain importing application/adapters/UI/skills/scripts and prevent application importing adapters/UI/skills/scripts.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/audit-drift.test.mjs covers domain->application and application->adapter violations; npm run check:architecture passes.

closure_brief.user_intent: Complete TASK-087 domain-led CodeWiki layer migration.
implemented_changes: Added domain modules for roadmap/task, change traceability, build lifecycle, session artifact status, validation profile taxonomy, and state sections.
Reused domain task-boundary, change traceability, and build lifecycle helpers from application code.
Added compiler wrapper modules for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation flows.
Added gateway profile wrapper modules for implementation and task-close validation.
non_goals_preserved: No user-facing behavior change except import/path compatibility.
remaining_risks: Large application modules can be further decomposed in future bounded tasks, but TASK-087 acceptance is met.

risks: Some compiler and gateway modules are wrappers around existing implementation rather than full internal decomposition, as allowed by TASK-087 acceptance.
Broader source modules such as builds.ts and graph.ts remain large and can be further split later, but required layer seams now exist with guards.

restore.note: Restored history is reference material until promoted into active knowledge or roadmap truth.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-087 Migrate source to domain-led CodeWiki layers implementation evidence
commit.body:
Completed TASK-087 domain-led layering migration through domain modules, compiler/gateway wrappers, dependency guards, and compatibility exports.

Checks: npm run typecheck, npm run check:architecture, npm test
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
